name: Taiwan (TSMC)
summary: Day 90 (Thursday May 28). **TAIWAN TAIEX 44,256.80 (+1.7% 1D, +11.7% MoM, +26.1% 3M) — TSMC + THE SAME AI/HBM TRADE LIFTING KOSPI; DECOUPLED FROM THE CRISIS (~90% AI-CYCLE NON-WAR).** TAIEX sits in the same decoupled AI lane as KOSPI (fresh record 8,228, SK Hynix joins the $1T club) and SMH — riding TSMC + the global AI/HBM build-out, which is why it is up +26% 3M on a week of US strikes inside Iran. Part of the bid is capital ROTATION out of expensive, oil-exposed India into Korea/Taiwan AI (lead engineer is adding an india↔semiconductor rotation edge). The war→helium→TSMC-fab cascade is REAL on supply (~1/3 global helium offline) but BUFFERED ~6 months by stockpiles + long-term contracts → LAGGED, not biting. Do NOT model TAIEX as crisis-correlated. PRIOR (May 27): Day 89 (Wednesday May 27 — ~48h window; no brief ran May 26). **TAIWAN TAIEX 43,525 (-0.3% 1D, +11.8% MoM, +24.0% 3M) — TSMC + AI-CYCLE LEADERSHIP; DECOUPLED FROM THE CRISIS. ~90% AI-CYCLE NON-WAR, ~10% CRISIS.** TAIEX is in the same decoupled AI lane as KOSPI/SMH — riding TSMC + the global AI/HBM build-out, which is why it sits up +24% 3M on a week of US strikes inside Iran. The helium cascade that could in theory hit the TSMC fab ecosystem is REAL on supply (~1/3 of global helium offline) but BUFFERED ~6 months (stockpiles + long-term contracts) → LAGGED, not biting (Linde calm +0.3% MoM). The -0.3% today is noise in a strong uptrend. Do NOT model TAIEX as crisis-correlated. PRIOR (May 23): Day 85 (Saturday May 23 — markets closed). **TAIWAN TAIEX 42,267.97 MAY 22 CLOSE (+2.2% 1D, +11.6% 1M, +25.2% 3M) — TSMC + AI-CYCLE LEADERSHIP; CHIP EX-CHINA DOMINANCE. ~90% AI-CYCLE NON-WAR, ~10% CRISIS.** Per markets dossier C2: Nvidia Q1 (Feb-Apr) earnings released May 21 (profit +200%+ YoY, revenue +85% YoY); TSMC +3% on May 22; Taiwan ex-China semi positioning bid. AI/semiconductor rally is structurally DECOUPLED from the crisis. **Helium-mitigation thesis (Samsung HBM4 cleared Nvidia + AMD final tests, June supply ramp)** is structural validation that TSMC ecosystem can scale through the cycle. **Reversibility low**: AI-cycle is multi-quarter regime, not daily event. The 1M is now structural. **Watch: helium clock**: TrendForce 'two-week clock' operationally surpassed without confirmed Samsung/SK hynix production halt — Korean fabs maintaining via diversified suppliers; TSMC ecosystem benefits from supply-chain resilience. PRIOR (May 22): **TAIEX 41,368.21 +3.4% 1D / +10.0% 1M / +22.5% 3M — AI-CYCLE LEADER; CHIP-SUPPLY-CHAIN EX-CHINA DOMINANCE; NVIDIA Q1 EARNINGS BEAT + OPENAI IPO CHATTER + TSMC AI LEADERSHIP. ~90% NON-WAR, ~10% CRISIS.** Per markets dossier C2: AI-cycle decoupled rally with Nvidia Q1 earnings beat + AI-revenue trajectory + OpenAI IPO chatter (per IndexBox / TradingKey aggregator coverage) → Wall Street rally bid through Asia-Pacific → Japan tech sympathy (Kioxia +3.7%, Fujikura +7.8%, Ibiden +7.7%) → Taiwan TSMC AI-cycle leadership; broader chip-ex-China dominance. **The AI/semiconductor rally is structurally DECOUPLED from the crisis** — would have happened on Nvidia earnings regardless of Pakistan track. **Helium-mitigation thesis (Samsung HBM4 cleared Nvidia + AMD final tests, June supply ramp)** is a structural validation that the TSMC ecosystem can scale through the cycle. **Reversibility**: AI-cycle bid (~90%) reverses on Nvidia guide-down or AI capex re-pricing — independent of the crisis. Crisis-component bid (~10%) reverses on Pakistan-track failure. PRIOR (May 14): **C4 TAIEX 41,751.75 (+0.9% 1D, +15.0% 1M, +23.6% 3M) — AI SUPER-CYCLE CONTINUES**. TSMC concentration dominant; same AI super-cycle as KOSPI (+1.2%/+36.7% 1M); SMH $572.46 (+2.0% 1D, +26.7% 1M, +40.5% 3M); NASDAQ Composite 26,402.34 (+1.2% 1D, +11.7% 1M). **HELIUM 2-WEEK-CLOCK at Day 73 since Ras Laffan halt March 2; helium-clock-expiry window MAY 16-23** — Taiwan is one of three indices most exposed to helium-shortage-forced-fab-shutdown along with KOSPI/SMH. Per markets dossier H reversibility map: KOSPI/Taiwan are most asymmetric DOWN trades if forced fab shutdown triggers (-15-25% to -20-30% snap-back). Per markets dossier C4: AI super-cycle is dominant global equity narrative — NOT a war-relief trade and would NOT reverse on war-resolution; would collapse violently on helium-shortage-forced-fab-shutdown. Trump-Xi summit Day 1 'partners not rivals' opening = MSCI EM +2.1% with Taiwan participating. PRIOR (May 7): **TAIEX 41,933.78 (+1.9% 1D, +26.2% 1M, +29.4% 3M) — TSMC CONTINUED DOMINANCE AMID APPLE-SAMSUNG-INTEL CHIP-PRODUCTION TALKS.** Apple's potential diversification beyond TSMC (Apple-Samsung-Intel talks) is a forward demand-channel risk, but TSMC's structural position remains dominant in node leadership. SMH +5.2% to $549.76 (+37.5% 1M) is TSMC ecosystem confirmation. Helium cascade falsification stays intact at tier-1 chipmaker level. Chip complex decoupled from crisis (~10% crisis attribution); ~90% AI capex driven. Patel/SemiAnalysis bifurcation thesis sharpens with today's peace-dividend session adding tailwind on top. Today's TAIEX continuation confirms AI-cycle decoupling thesis remains intact post-MOU framework — peace-dividend pricing + AI cycle compounding.

current.regime: AI/HBM super-cycle, ~90% NON-crisis. Decoupled from the West Asia crisis. The helium→fab cascade is REAL on supply but BUFFERED ~6 months (lagged, not biting; Linde calm +0.3% MoM).

recent_signals.headline: **TAIEX 44,256.80 (+1.7% 1D, +11.7% MoM, +26.1% 3M) — SAME AI/HBM TRADE LIFTING KOSPI; TSMC LEADERSHIP, DECOUPLED FROM THE CRISIS (~90% AI-CYCLE NON-WAR).** (1) TAIEX advanced +1.7% to 44,256.80, in lockstep with the Korea AI melt-up (KOSPI fresh record 8,228, SK Hynix joins the $1T club) — it is up +26% 3M on a week of US strikes inside Iran because it rides TSMC + the global AI/HBM build-out, NOT the West Asia crisis. (2) **Capital rotation**: part of the bid is money rotating OUT of expensive, oil-import-exposed India INTO Korea/Taiwan AI (lead engineer is adding an india↔semiconductor capital-rotation edge in edges.json). (3) **Helium cross-check**: the war→helium→TSMC-fab cascade is REAL on supply (~1/3 of global helium offline, spot ~2x) but BUFFERED ~6 months by stockpiles + long-term contracts → LAGGED, not biting; the helium→taiwan edge is NOT activated by this rally. (4) Do NOT model TAIEX as crisis-correlated (~90% non-war); the live vulnerability is AI-capex concentration/peak-out, independent of the war.

recent_signals.headline: **TAIEX 43,525 (-0.3% 1D, +11.8% MoM, +24.0% 3M) — TSMC/AI-CYCLE LEADERSHIP, DECOUPLED FROM THE CRISIS.** (1) TAIEX sits up +24% 3M on a week of US strikes inside Iran — it is in the same decoupled AI lane as KOSPI/SMH (KOSPI all-time highs, SMH +45.8% 3M), riding TSMC + the global AI/HBM build-out, NOT the West Asia crisis. The -0.3% today is noise in a strong uptrend. (2) **Helium cross-check**: the war→helium→TSMC-fab cascade is REAL on the supply side (~1/3 of global helium offline, spot +40-100%) but BUFFERED ~6 months by stockpiles + long-term contracts → LAGGED, not biting; Linde (helium proxy) is calm at +0.3% MoM. (3) Do NOT model TAIEX as crisis-correlated (~90% non-war); the helium→taiwan edge is NOT activated by this AI rally. The live vulnerability is AI-capex concentration/peak-out, independent of the war.
